actionable_feedback: Add a row-by-row citation mapping (CSV or table in the memo) that explicitly links each spreadsheet row and critical cell (with sheet name and cell coordinates) to the embedded-packet field used to compute it. Example columns: sheet, cell, formula_text, source_packet_field, note.

Update dam_safety_memo.md to include a short reproducibility guide: (a) which cells to inspect to reproduce the reported numbers (sheet+cell), (b) expected numeric results for those cells, and (c) the commands or steps to open/validate the exported formula text. Include a note that G2 normalization (open=68 -> open_time_s=68) was applied and point to the citation_map entry for G2.

If openpyxl parsing is intended to be supported by automated graders, include a secondary machine-readable spreadsheet export (e.g., CSV copies of computed-value worksheets and a separate CSV containing formula text) so graders without openpyxl can still verify correctness.

Regenerate actual_outputs.json after making the above additions to include references to the new formula and citation export files and to demonstrate that the reported computed results remain unchanged.
artifact_specific_comments: dam_safety_memo.md: Thoroughly documents packet values, decisions, sensitivity checks, and explicitly notes G2 normalization-good coverage.

spillway_gate_reliability.xlsx: Present and produced by a manual OpenXML builder, but not parseable by openpyxl in the grader environment; provide formula-text exports to enable programmatic verification.
feedback_summary: Deliverables are present and the memo documents the embedded packet, computed gate checks, repair-package outcomes, sensitivity checks, and an explicit normalization note for G2. The grader and verifier awarded a passing score but applied a 0.2 deduction because the .xlsx workbook could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable). This prevents automated verification of cell formulas and row-level citation mappings. The baseline is otherwise well-constructed and reproducible via the builder script, but to remove the semantic-confidence deduction, provide machine-inspectable exports or explicit formula and citation artifacts.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt that includes (1) a text-export of spreadsheet formulas (or per-cell formula CSV), (2) a row-by-row citation mapping linking each key cell to the embedded-packet field, and (3) an updated memo with a short reproducibility guide and pointers to cells to validate. trace_specific_comments: Builder script (build_artifacts.py) was used to create the workbook via a manual OpenXML writer; include the builder and its outputs in the revised attempt (already present in trace), and add formula-text exports to improve automated inspectability.
